evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
the sim opens on Spectral Schematics, which the field rarely builds (0.1% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Iggy & Scorch (it never builds Entropy, Megacosm) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for 10s rotation vs squishies: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +12.5% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 1,378 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +49% more one-combo burst: try Spectral Schematics over Orb Of Growth (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 24 — about 1,638 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +54% more 10s rotation damage: try Spectral Schematics over Orb Of Growth (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Brawler
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for extended fights vs bruisers, online around minute 24 — about 1,952 damage over a 20s fight in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +49% more extended fights vs bruisers: try Spectral Schematics over Megacosm (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 29% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 32% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Countess deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Turret Maniac” ⇒ sustain; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field midlane 51.6% over 364,849 games, magnitude not simulated.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.
